name: Natural Promenade Turn
preceding_figure_names: Basic Reverse Turn, Chase, Contra Check, Fallaway Four Step, Fallaway Promenade, Five Step, Four Step
following_figure_names: Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade, Rock Turn (2-7)
notes: Amount of Turn: 3/4 to right. If danced along the side of the room, 3/4 still made; following promenade may be taken to the center or a slight body turn to R may be used. When followed by Rock Turn, 4th step omitted and commence DC; better to turn 1/2 to R between 1-3 of Rock Turn and end facing DW. Track man's hips, stay behind; depending on finishing travel direction, lady may need to swivel on LF (S) quite a bit. When followed by steps 2-7 Rock Turn, skip step 4 (placing RF to side), it is more flat and rotational. Natural Promenade Turn follows the Fallaway Promenade. Alignment details: Leader: DW pointing to W; Q: To Wall; Q: BDW; S: Down LOD end facing LOD. Follower: S: DW pointing LOD; Q: Pointing DW; Q: DW; S: BLOD to end facing Center.

steps.action: to side in PP, along LOD, pointing DW
comments: Don't pull lady to your lady, instead Man should switch lane.

steps.action: forward in PP and CBMP, turning R, CBM
to side and slightly back, backing LOD, turning R
forward in CBMP, LF placed to side without weight in PP
comments: Note: Amount of Turn: 3/4 to right.

steps.action: to side in PP, along LOD, pointing DC
forward and across in PP and CBMP, pointing down LOD
comments: Really follow/track Man's leg in time (if it's off time, it'll gap.).

steps.action: forward between partner's feet, down LOD, CBM
to side and slightly back, RF placed to side without weight in PP
comments: Depending on how much Man rotates, where he puts his foot, Lady will put down foot just a bit late. Because body is turning R, LF appears to be to side and slightly back, then swivel on LF again to Promenade finishing position.
